div1.entryFree:
Flash, vb. 1) to burst on the sight with a sudden blaze: --ing fire will follow, H5 II, 1, 56. Trans.: it --ed forth fire, as lightning from the sky, Ven. 348.
2) to shine, to dazzle the eye: which --es now a Phoenix, Tim. II, 1, 32.
3) to break out: he --es into one gross crime or other, Lr. I, 3, 4.
